title: Odd Lots: Grace Shao on How Chinese AI is Different (Podcast)
summary: China’s AI industry has changed a lot since DeepSeek released its cheap frontier model last year, and briefly sent US tech stocks falling. After being locked out of the most advanced chips, Chinese companies are now allowed to buy some Nvidia H200s. In fact, many of the big Chinese tech companies — like Baidu — are making a push to become full-stack players, with their own chips, models, and cloud infrastructure. title: Thai Shipper Remains Wary of Hormuz Despite Ceasefire
summary: Bangkok-based Precious Shipping Pcl, whose vessel was struck in March by Iranian projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz, said it remains cautious about sending ships through one of the world’s most important energy and trade chokepoints.

title: Rupee May Get Further Boost as Indian Banks Line Up Overseas Debt
summary: A Nifty gauge of lenders has risen about 6% since June 5.

title: Pound, Gilts Rise After UK Prime Minister Starmer Resigns
summary: The pound and gilts edged higher after UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigned and the path to power began to clear for the frontrunner to succeed him.

title: Emerging-Market Stocks Mark Fresh High as US-Iran Talks Progress, Oil Falls
summary: Emerging-market stocks touched a fresh all-time high on signs of progress in US-Iran peace talks, with Asia’s technology giants extending their rally as investors continued to pour money into companies linked to artificial intelligence.

title: EasyJet Rebuffs Castlelake Bid That’s Well Above Share Price
summary: EasyJet Plc has rebuffed three separate offers from Castlelake LP, prompting the investment firm to take its proposal valuing the budget carrier at about £4.74 billion ($6.3 billion) directly to shareholders.

title: Portugal Eyes Wealth Fund for Strategic Stakes, Premier Says
summary: The Portuguese government plans to create a sovereign wealth fund that would allow the state to retain stakes in strategic sectors of the economy.

title: Stocks Dip Despite Drop in Oil on US-Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
summary: US stock futures edged lower, finding little support from weaker oil prices following signs of diplomatic headway between the US and Iran.

title: ‘Buy Europe’ Trade Returns as Stagflation Fears Ease: Markets Daily
summary: Bets on easing inflation and stronger economic growth are boosting the region’s stocks.

title: UK Bonds Welcome Lower Odds of Prolonged Leadership Battle
Europe Heat Wave Intensifies With France and UK on Red Alert
summary: A heat wave gripping western Europe is forecast to strengthen this week, with days of dangerously hot and humid conditions and record-breaking temperatures in France, the UK, Spain and Germany.

title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Quits | The Pulse 6/22/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Gilles Moëc, Axa Chief Economist; John Aylward, Sona Asset Management Founder & CIO; Anand Menon, UK In A Changing Europe Director; Greg Fleming, Rockefeller Capital Management President & CEO (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Treasuries Decline as Trump’s Iran Threats Stoke Inflation Fears
summary: Treasuries fell after President Donald Trump again threatened military action against Iran over Hezbollah’s attacks on Israel, prompting investors to reassess inflation risks.

title: JPMorgan’s Lipikhina Sees Europe Stocks Lagging Despite Oil Drop
summary: European equities may struggle to keep pace with US and emerging market peers in the second half of the year as falling oil prices threaten a key source of earnings growth, according to Nataliia Lipikhina at JPMorgan Chase & Co.

title: Oil Declines After US-Iran Peace Talks Show Signs of Progress
summary: Oil declined as Iran said there had been progress in peace talks with the US, bolstering hopes for a recovery in flows from the Persian Gulf.

title: Gold Advances After Negotiators Flag Headway in US-Iran Talks
summary: Gold rose after negotiators heralded progress in talks between the US and Iran to find a permanent resolution to the war that’s roiled global markets.

title: France, Germany Agree on Stakes in Tankmaker KNDS Before IPO
summary: The German and French governments are set to hold equal stakes in KNDS NV, one of Europe’s most important defense companies, as it gears up for an initial public offering in the near future.

title: China Lays Out Blueprint to Boost Consumption of Clean Energy
summary: China has laid out broad new rules that mandate increased use of renewables as it continues to shift the focus of its energy transition from building new generation to ensuring it gets consumed.

title: Lithium Plunges in China on Speculation Over Status of CATL Mine
summary: Chinese lithium futures have shed about 9% in two days on unconfirmed speculation one of the world’s biggest mines for the battery metal may restart soon.

title: Korea Markets Chief Regrets Allowing Single-Stock Leveraged ETFs
summary: South Korea’s top financial regulator said he regrets not blocking the launch of leveraged exchange-traded funds tracking single stocks last month, warning their negative side effects have grown significantly.

title: Pound Falls to Trade Near 2026 Low as UK’s Starmer Resigns
summary: The pound traded near this year’s low as Keir Starmer said he would step down as Britain’s prime minister, with traders weighing whether a new Labour administration could revive concerns over the UK’s fiscal outlook.

title: Godiva Japan Said to Seek Extension on $464 Million LBO Loan
summary: Godiva Japan Inc. is in talks with its banking syndicate to extend the repayment deadline on leveraged buyout financing as the chocolate maker struggles in the major Asian market, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Collapsed Lender MFS Said to Have Never Registered £300 Million of Mortgages
summary: A £300 million ($396 million) portfolio of mortgages linked to collapsed lender Market Financial Solutions Ltd. looks to never have been registered with public property records, according to court filings, threatening to complicate lenders’ efforts to establish their claims over the underlying assets.

title: Qatar Brings Empty LNG Ships Through Hormuz as Exports Rise
summary: Qatar is rushing to bring home more empty liquefied natural gas tankers as the nation prepares to restart shipments accounting for about a fifth of global supply.

title: Soaring Profits in Emerging Markets Build Case for a Raging Bull Market
summary: For the first time in four years, companies in emerging markets are beating profit estimates, giving investors a fresh reason to believe the bull market is just getting started.

title: Iranian Crude Exports Surge Via Hormuz as Activity Picks Up
summary: Iran has ramped up the amount of oil it sends openly through the Strait of Hormuz to the highest since the war began, as shipping activity picks up while Tehran and Washington seek a lasting peace deal.

title: Australian Spot Power Price Jumps on Still and Cloudy Weather
summary: A key spot price in Australia’s electricity market jumped as calm conditions curbed wind turbine output, highlighting weather’s growing role as a driver of power market volatility.

title: EasyJet Rejects Castlelake Takeover Bids
summary: EasyJet has rejected three separate offers from Castlelake, prompting the investment firm to take its latest proposal that values the budget carrier at about £4.74 billion ($6.3 billion) directly to shareholders. The UK airline said it rejected the proposal as not being in the best interest of shareholders, accusing Castlelake of trying to buy the company "on the cheap." Bloomberg's Benedikt Kammel reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Escrivá Says ECB Must Be Vigilant on Oil Price Impact on Wages
summary: The European Central Bank must watch for any knock-on impact on wages from higher oil and commodity prices resulting from the Middle East conflict, Governing Council member Jose Luis Escrivá said.

title: Japan’s Push for Corporate Growth Sets Off Skepticism
summary: The Japanese government’s new push to encourage companies to use their cash for longer-term growth is raising concerns it will lead to unprofitable investments and erode corporate value.

title: Bonds Under Pressure Despite Oil Relief: 3-Minutes MLIV
summary: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson, Tom Mackenzie and Mark Cudmore break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)
